name: Audit and Fix Dependency Vulnerabilities
description: Scan your project for known security vulnerabilities in dependencies, understand severity levels, and apply fixes safely.
trendingReason: Supply chain security is a top priority as dependency-based attacks increase in frequency

steps.title: Run a security audit
steps.description: Scan all dependencies for known vulnerabilities.

steps.title: View detailed audit report as JSON
steps.description: Get machine-readable output for CI integration or detailed analysis.
steps.tip: Pipe to jq for filtering: 'npm audit --json | jq '.vulnerabilities | to_entries[] | select(.value.severity=="critical")'

steps.title: Auto-fix compatible vulnerabilities
steps.description: Automatically update packages to patched versions within semver range.
steps.warning: npm audit fix only updates within the current semver range. It will not make breaking changes to your dependency tree.

steps.title: Force-fix with major version updates
steps.description: Apply fixes that may include breaking semver changes.
steps.warning: Always use --dry-run first to review changes. Force-fixing can introduce breaking changes that require code updates.

steps.title: Check audit in CI with exit codes
steps.description: Fail CI builds when critical or high vulnerabilities are found.
steps.tip: Use --audit-level to set the minimum severity that causes a non-zero exit code: info, low, moderate, high, critical.

faq.question: What if npm audit fix does not resolve all vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: Some vulnerabilities require major version bumps of transitive dependencies that npm cannot safely auto-fix. In these cases, check if the vulnerability affects your usage, file an issue with the package maintainer, or use overrides in package.json to force a specific version.

faq.question: How do I ignore a specific vulnerability?
faq.answer: There is no built-in ignore mechanism in npm audit. Use the 'overrides' field in package.json to pin a transitive dependency to a patched version, or use a tool like better-npm-audit that supports allowlists.

faq.question: Should I run npm audit in production CI?
faq.answer: Yes. Add 'npm audit --audit-level=critical' to your CI pipeline to block deployments with critical vulnerabilities. Use --audit-level=high for stricter policies. Consider --omit=dev to only audit production dependencies.
